summary: Phase 1 Plaid backend testing complete. All 17 pytest cases pass (100%) covering status, link-token, sandbox/simulate (idempotent), items, transactions, summary aggregates, refresh, webhook (no-auth), delete, AES-256-GCM encryption round-trip, and regression on /api/travel/trips & /api/auth/me. Sandbox seed fallback works end-to-end without real Plaid credentials — 1 item, 3 accounts (Checking $2847.50 / Savings $4200 / CC $410), 9 seeded transactions matching PLOS profile (GSU payroll x2, EBT, mortgage, Chase CC pmt, Georgia Power, AT&T, Kroger, Murphy Express). Summary math validated: income_30d=6165.00, expenses_30d=2296.63, monthly_surplus=3868.37, total_balance=7047.50, credit_debt=410.00, sandbox=true. Access token encryption tag verified (bad-AAD raises). No /api/finance/snapshot endpoint exists in codebase; substituted /api/auth/me + /api/travel/trips for regression sanity.

minor.issue: Plaid router is mounted TWICE at server.py:6274-6276 — once via `api_router.include_router(make_plaid_router(...))` (prefix /api) and again via `app.include_router(make_plaid_router(...), prefix="/api")`. Both resolve to the same path so first match wins, but it registers duplicate routes and duplicate notify hooks may run. Recommend removing the second mount.

action_items: Remove duplicate Plaid router mount at server.py:6275-6276 (app.include_router of make_plaid_router with prefix='/api') — api_router already includes it.

When user provides real PLAID_CLIENT_ID / PLAID_SECRET_SANDBOX, re-run these tests against real Plaid sandbox; the tests currently pass only because HAS_REAL_KEYS is false (sandbox-seed short-circuit). Real-key tests will need Plaid public_token mocking or Plaid sandbox_public_token_create.
Consider adding /api/finance/snapshot alias (mentioned in review request but not present in codebase) or clarify with user.
critical_code_review_comments: plaid_service.py:474 — webhook handler correctly excludes auth (public endpoint) as required by Plaid; good.
plaid_service.py:390 — GET /plaid/items projection correctly excludes access_token_enc from response; verified.
plaid_service.py:481-483 — sandbox_simulate idempotency check on {user_id, sandbox_seed:true} works correctly and returns already_exists=true on 2nd call.
plaid_service.py:52-63 — AES-256-GCM uses random 12-byte IV per encryption and AAD binding to user_id — solid implementation.
server.py:6274-6276 — DUPLICATE mount of Plaid router (see minor issue above).
seed_data_creation: Tests self-seed via POST /api/plaid/sandbox/simulate and clean up both pre- and post-run via DELETE /api/plaid/items/{id}. No manual seeding required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Plaid Phase 1 sandbox-seed mode fully validated. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_plaid_service.py is reusable. When Phase 2+ adds real Plaid credentials, HAS_REAL_KEYS will flip true and several assertions (sandbox_fallback=true, sandbox=true, sandbox_seed=true, accounts_synced==3) will need to be gated behind `if not HAS_REAL_KEYS`. The autouse cleanup fixture only removes sandbox_seed items so real Plaid items would survive across runs — adjust accordingly.
